articles.title: Rochdale Council Proposes New Dog Fouling Public Space Protection Order
articles.excerpt: Rochdale Borough Council has proposed a new Public Space Protection Order aimed at tackling dog fouling in the area, following concerns over cleanliness and public health.
articles.content_html:
Rochdale Borough Council has proposed a new Public Space Protection Order (PSPO) to address dog fouling in the area. The initiative aims to improve cleanliness and public health.

The proposed PSPO is in accordance with the Anti-Social Behaviour, Crime and Policing Act 2014, Section 59. It seeks to provide the council with additional powers to manage dog fouling effectively.

If implemented, the order could lead to increased fines for dog owners who fail to clean up after their pets.

The council is currently seeking feedback on the proposal, with further details available in the document published on their website.
articles.legal_disclaimer: This article was compiled from identified public sources and may be updated when further verified information becomes available.

articles.title: St. Edmund’s Church in Castleton Dates Back to the 11th Century
articles.excerpt: St. Edmund’s Church, located in Castleton, has historical roots dating back to the 11th and 12th centuries, showcasing its significance in the area.
articles.content_html:
St. Edmund’s Church in Castleton has historical origins that date back to the 11th and 12th centuries. This church is a notable landmark in the area, reflecting the architectural and cultural heritage of the time.

The church has served the local community for centuries, playing a vital role in the spiritual life of Castleton.

Visitors to St. Edmund’s can appreciate its historical significance and the craftsmanship of its early architecture.

As a part of Castleton's heritage, St. Edmund’s Church continues to be a focal point for community events and gatherings.

articles.title: Average Asking Rent in Rochdale Reaches Record High
articles.excerpt: The average asking rent in Rochdale has reached a record high, reflecting ongoing trends in the housing market.
articles.content_html:
A recent report indicates that the average asking rent in Rochdale has reached a record high. This development highlights the increasing demand for rental properties in the area.

The rise in rental prices is attributed to various factors, including a shortage of available properties and growing interest in the region.

Local residents may find that this increase in rental costs could impact their housing options, making it more challenging to secure affordable accommodation.

articles.title: Rochdale Students Win National Award for Online Safety Film
articles.excerpt: Students from Skills 4 All in Rochdale have secured second place in the secondary category of the 2026 Childnet Film Competition with a film on the responsible use of artificial intelligence.
articles.content_html:
A group of students from Skills 4 All in Rochdale has won second place in the secondary category of the 2026 Childnet Film Competition. Their film addresses the safe and responsible use of artificial intelligence (AI).

The awards were announced at Disney headquarters in Hammersmith during the 17th annual Childnet Film Competition, which invites young people across the UK to create short films promoting online safety. This year's theme was 'Smart tech, safe choices', focusing on the implications of AI in everyday life. The students' film is set in a dystopian version of Manchester, illustrating the potential consequences of over-reliance on AI.

The judging panel, which included representatives from the BBC, the British Board of Film Classification, Disney, and the Motion Picture Association, commended the film for its creativity and engaging storytelling. As runners-up, the students will receive professional filmmaking equipment, and their film will be classified by the BBFC, allowing it to be screened in cinemas across the UK.

Will Gardner OBE, chief executive of Childnet, highlighted the importance of educating young people about the responsible use of AI, noting that 97% of children aged 8 to 17 have used AI in some form. Charlotte Tudor, vice president of marketing for Disney, expressed pride in supporting the initiative, emphasising the need for safe online experiences.
